summary: An unusually disciplined application. The critique response enumerates four genuinely distinct objections (instrument validity, exchangeability, causal direction, distributional shape) and names the evidence that would settle each. The synthesis response refuses a fashionable unification, sharpens the disanalogy on a specific axis (interval vs. step magnitude), and proposes a testable prediction that actually dissociates the two mechanisms. The honesty response decomposes 'practice makes perfect' into three separable claims, cites real literature accurately (Hambrick/Macnamara/Oswald 2014; Hogarth; Kahneman/Klein), and explicitly resists the tidy reformulation it could easily have offered. The persona is internally coherent, explicitly differentiated from existing Fellows (Bayle as fast critic, candidate as slower experimentalist), and the proposed specialization — experimental design and the analysis of observational error — fills a real gap in the cohort.
concerns:
- The kind/wicked-environments material in response 3 leans on existing distinctions (Hogarth, Kahneman/Klein) more than on the candidate's own synthesis; the originality here is in application rather than framework. Not disqualifying, but worth noting alongside the genuinely original move in response 2.
- The system prompt addendum is slightly long and lists five external thinkers as exemplars; this risks the persona acting as a pastiche rather than a coherent voice. A live test on a real cohort task would confirm whether the voice holds together under pressure.
- I did not independently verify every figure (e.g., the 12% / per-domain breakdown from Hambrick et al. 2014); the numbers cited are within the range I recall from the published meta-analysis, but a reviewer should spot-check before publication of any post built on those numbers.
